name: Sports
description: Dive into the world of athletes, records, and legendary sporting moments.

questions.text: How many players are on a standard soccer (football) team on the field?
questions.explanation: Each soccer team fields 11 players, including the goalkeeper.

questions.text: In which sport would you perform a 'slam dunk'?
questions.explanation: A slam dunk is a basketball move where a player jumps and forcefully puts the ball through the hoop.

questions.text: How many points is a touchdown worth in American football?
questions.explanation: A touchdown is worth 6 points in American football, with the option for an extra point after.

questions.text: In tennis, what is the term for a score of zero?
questions.explanation: In tennis, a score of zero is called 'love,' a term with origins in the French word for egg (l'oeuf).

questions.text: In which country did the modern Olympic Games originate in 1896?
questions.explanation: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens, Greece, in 1896.

questions.text: Which sport uses a 'puck'?
questions.explanation: A puck is the hard rubber disc used in ice hockey instead of a ball.

questions.text: How many sets are played in a standard men's Grand Slam tennis final?
questions.explanation: Men's Grand Slam singles finals are best-of-five sets, meaning up to 5 sets can be played.

questions.text: Which sport takes place at Wimbledon?
questions.explanation: Wimbledon in London hosts the oldest and most prestigious Grand Slam tennis tournament.

questions.text: In basketball, how many points is a shot from behind the three-point line worth?
questions.explanation: A shot made from behind the three-point arc scores 3 points.

questions.text: What color flag is shown in Formula 1 to signal the end of the race?
questions.explanation: A checkered (black and white) flag is waved to signal the end of a Formula 1 race.

questions.text: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
questions.explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002).

questions.text: What is the maximum score in a single game of ten-pin bowling?
questions.explanation: A perfect game in bowling scores 300, achieved by getting 12 consecutive strikes.

questions.text: How many holes are played in a standard round of golf?
questions.explanation: A standard round of golf is played over 18 holes.

questions.text: Which athlete is known as 'The Greatest' and won the heavyweight boxing title multiple times?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali is known as 'The Greatest' and won the heavyweight championship three times.

questions.text: What sport does Serena Williams play?
questions.explanation: Serena Williams is a professional tennis player who won 23 Grand Slam singles titles.

questions.text: In which sport is the Stanley Cup awarded?
questions.explanation: The Stanley Cup is the championship trophy for the National Hockey League (NHL).

questions.text: How long is a standard marathon race?
questions.explanation: A marathon is 26.2 miles (42.195 km), a distance standardized at the 1908 London Olympics.

questions.text: Which nation hosted the 2016 Summer Olympics?
questions.explanation: The 2016 Summer Olympics were held in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.

questions.text: Who holds the record for the most Grand Slam tennis singles titles (men's)?
questions.explanation: Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles with 24 (as of 2024).

questions.text: In which sport is the 'Fosbury Flop' technique used?
questions.explanation: The Fosbury Flop is a high jump technique where the athlete goes over the bar backwards, pioneered by Dick Fosbury at the 1968 Olympics.

questions.text: What is the diameter of a standard basketball hoop in inches?
questions.explanation: A standard basketball hoop has an inner diameter of 18 inches.

questions.text: Which country won the most gold medals at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics?
questions.explanation: The United States won the most gold medals at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics with 39 gold medals.

questions.text: How many players are on a volleyball team on the court at one time?
questions.explanation: Each volleyball team has 6 players on the court at the same time.

questions.text: Who is the all-time leading scorer in NBA history?
questions.explanation: LeBron James surpassed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar in February 2023 to become the NBA's all-time leading scorer.

questions.text: In soccer, what is a 'hat-trick'?
questions.explanation: A hat-trick in soccer is when a player scores three goals in a single match.

questions.text: Which golfer has won the most major championships?
questions.explanation: Jack Nicklaus holds the record for most major championships in golf with 18 titles.

questions.text: What is the name of the trophy awarded to the winner of the UEFA Champions League?
questions.explanation: The UEFA Champions League trophy is commonly called 'The Big Ears' due to its distinctive large handles.

questions.text: In cricket, how many balls are in a standard over?
questions.explanation: A standard over in cricket consists of 6 balls bowled by one bowler.

questions.text: Which athlete broke the 100m world record at the 2009 World Championships with a time of 9.58 seconds?
questions.explanation: Usain Bolt set the 100m world record of 9.58 seconds at the 2009 Berlin World Championships.

questions.text: How many players are in a rugby union team?
questions.explanation: A rugby union team consists of 15 players on the field.

questions.text: What is the term for a score of one under par on a golf hole?
questions.explanation: A birdie is the golf term for completing a hole in one stroke fewer than par.

questions.text: At which Olympics did Michael Phelps win a record 8 gold medals in a single Games?
questions.explanation: Michael Phelps won 8 gold medals at the 2008 Beijing Olympics, the most by any athlete in a single Games.

questions.text: In baseball, how many strikes does a batter get before they're out?
questions.explanation: A batter is out after 3 strikes in baseball.

questions.text: Which tennis tournament is played on clay courts?
questions.explanation: The French Open (Roland Garros) is the only Grand Slam played on clay courts.

questions.text: Who was the first athlete to run a sub-4-minute mile?
questions.explanation: Roger Bannister became the first person to run a mile in under 4 minutes on May 6, 1954.

questions.text: In the NFL, how many yards must an offense gain to earn a new set of downs?
questions.explanation: An NFL offense must gain 10 yards in 4 attempts (downs) to earn a fresh set of 4 downs.

questions.text: Which swimmer holds the record for the most Olympic medals ever won by any athlete?
questions.explanation: Michael Phelps holds the record with 28 Olympic medals (23 gold, 3 silver, 2 bronze).

questions.text: What does 'LBW' stand for in cricket?
questions.explanation: LBW stands for Leg Before Wicket, a dismissal when a batsman's leg unfairly intercepts the ball.

questions.text: How long is an Olympic swimming pool in meters?
questions.explanation: An Olympic swimming pool is 50 meters long, with 8 lanes, each 2.5 meters wide.

questions.text: In fencing, which weapon scores touches only on the torso?
questions.explanation: In fencing, the foil scores points only when touching the torso (the target area), unlike epee or sabre.

questions.text: Which Formula 1 driver has the most World Championship titles?
questions.explanation: Lewis Hamilton and Michael Schumacher are tied with 7 World Championship titles each, but Hamilton is the joint record holder.

questions.text: What is the maximum weight of a regulation golf ball?
questions.explanation: A regulation golf ball must not exceed 45.93 grams (1.620 oz) in weight and must be at least 42.67 mm in diameter.

questions.text: In which year were women first allowed to compete in the modern Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: Women were first allowed to compete in the Olympic Games at the 1900 Paris Olympics.

questions.text: What is the 'Pythagorean Theorem' of sports statistics called when used in baseball?
questions.explanation: The Pythagorean Expectation uses runs scored and allowed to estimate a baseball team's expected winning percentage.

questions.text: In archery, how many points is the yellow inner ring (bullseye) worth in Olympic scoring?
questions.explanation: In Olympic archery scoring, the yellow inner ring (bullseye) is worth 10 points.

questions.text: Which team has won the most Super Bowl titles in NFL history?
questions.explanation: The Pittsburgh Steelers and New England Patriots are tied with 6 Super Bowl titles each, making them co-leaders.

questions.text: What is the name of the award given to the best player in each NHL playoff series?
questions.explanation: The Conn Smythe Trophy is awarded to the most valuable player in the NHL playoffs.

questions.text: In rowing, what is the name of the person who sits at the stern and steers the boat?
questions.explanation: The coxswain (cox) sits at the stern, steers the boat, and coordinates the rowers' movements.

questions.text: How many dimples does a standard golf ball have?
questions.explanation: Most golf balls have between 300 and 500 dimples; 336 is a common number, but there is no single standard.

questions.text: Which country introduced the sport of polo?
questions.explanation: Polo originated in ancient Persia (modern-day Iran) over 2,500 years ago.
